channel.title: Comments for page "Person of Interest File #2273/01"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Person of Interest File #2273/01"

item.title: Re: Hey a review for once
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe I didn't respond to this.

It's only a supplement if you squint. This was written quite a while after the original SCP. It's not meant as a supplemental document, it's meant as an exploration and continuation of his story.

Regardless, I'm glad you liked it.
https://youtu.be/RlbARlVyRAc

item.title: Hey a review for once
item.content:encoded:
Hello,

It's been a hot minute since I've written about a piece, so let's change that!

To preface, I have a lot of bias against Supplemental/Additional files on skip pages. Sometimes they work, but for me I like everything being there on one nice package. (Barring linked canon tales, hubs. etc.)

This is one article that breaks that cycle. I enjoyed this one. A lot.

It both adds a nice addition to the original article, giving 2273 a very nice piece of growth. The formatting of the document also adds a bunch of charm; I don't think that the piece would've worked had it only been Alexei's POV the whole time.

Additionally, this works extremely standalone as well. You don't need to have read the original file, but having done so is rewarding as hell.

Thematically, I think the story works. The growth of a solider and his years of atonement are really good themes to have, and this article executes that nicely. His ending letter adds the last bit of flavor and growth.

Good job on the work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a minute since I've been truly involved in the site. Are happy/bittersweet endings really all that rare?

Unrelated note: epic username.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An article that's actually heartwarming. Very rare indeed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm always happy to see my articles have the positive impact I was going for, and I'm always amazed at comments such as this.

As a very much not religious person, this was meant as an atheist's love letter to religion, among other things. Religion can motivate astonishing beauty and selflessness but it can also motivate horror and horrific acts; I didn't feel up to writing criticism of religion when I was writing this but I might write something with the opposite approach to religion eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't usually comment. This is maybe my fifth comment on this site, over the many years I've been reading it.
I feel compelled to congratulate the author, on building such a touching story. You've managed to stick to the clinical tone of the site perfectly, while also pulling at the heart strings in a deep, meaningful way.
I was sad when Dr. Friedrich tragically died. Even before reading all of that, I was elated to read that Alexei didn't die in containment. That he found some semblance of peace in himself.
I am an atheist, but even the religious themes were moving.
Can I +2? Is that possible? +5?
I rarely even +1. But this is a masterpiece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God this is tugging at my heartstrings

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have my blessing and my encouragement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will have to see if I can fit it into a future tale/article I write. ONE of these days, I will attempt to write an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tend to research this sort of thing more thoroughly, sorry for this error. I'm on mobile, can't make the edit atm. I give authorial permission to anyone on the site to make this edit if I don't get 'round to it in the next 24 hours

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the article, juts have a tine nitpick (easely changable and not really that important)

Excerpt from a journal entry dated 25 December 2034: …Today is the day that our monastery celebrates the birth of our Lord…

I just thought Eastern Clockwork Orthodox Church should follow actual Eastern Orthodox traditions, I think that will be a really nice touch. Christmas is celebrated on the 7th of January by orthodox christians, so if you just move that date to two weeks later it's gonna look more authentic!

Again, love everything else about it, great job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used this same format as addenda in SCP-2739 and am not opposed to other authors adopting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can Person of Interest files be a new thing? I want to see/read more of these.

Definitely interesting to see this character out in the world and his interactions with the Clockwork and MCF as well as his relationships with personnel/SCPs. Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same, man. It's basically the same dynamic as a Big Daddy and Little Sister from BioShock and I'm sad that no one's done the fanart yet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My gawd.

I've got a pang in my heart.

Now I just need fanarts of 2273 and 191.

Uhhhhhhhhh…

Holy crap

item.title: Double replying because raisins
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, my initial reply was rude and dismissive.

This story is part of a continuity where anomalies become common, public knowledge. It's a world where the Foundation's modus operandi becomes invalid. The Foundation is outed for the global criminal empire it is. It's forced to accept outside governance. To save face, they release everything they trust not to cause humanity's extinction. This includes mild tempered humanoids like Alexei.

Sorry it's not your cup of tea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, and welcome to our community!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the post that made me finally join this site just so I could +1 it. It's an amazing document with a great voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's called lifted veil sweaty 😙😉

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even… huh? Since when does the Foundation release extradimensional supersoldiers in yeti-crafted bug armor out of their cells, standing on the sidelines while MCF takes them in? The old Foundation crankypants guys would (probably rightly) expect that MCF would try to find and free SCP-1000s, or the armor to start multiplying, or the radio to indoctrinate all nearby Russians. I like Alexei but this seems wildly out of character for the Foundation, even given their often fluctuating presentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it kind of is. It's 100% in-universe documentation, same as most SCP files. And, in-universe, there's probably a filing cabinet with hundreds of manilla folders, and one of the bigger folders is labelled "PoI #2273/01" and it has his old Special Containment Procedures document, all of his interviews, all of his scientific tests, all of his incident reports, and everything else tangentially related to him. This document is at the front of the folder so that folks digging through the archive have the most recent information on him.

Regardless, I'm happy you like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was it bad that I originally thought this was an SCP-2273 supplement? Either way, this builds on 2273 so much, and I love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually subsist on feels. Your tears sustain me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamnit MacLeod, you made me tear up.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, this is…fucking shit.

Every time I come back to this discussion page and see that, I think it looks like you're saying that this is absolutely sub-par, but in a really rude way. It always takes me a minute to realize that's not what you're saying lol.

As for the rest, I appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, this is…fucking shit. I knew the name looked familiar.

SCP-2273 is one of my favourite SCPs, (and is 100% my favourite in terms of character and story) and, gosh darn it this is…amazing. It's a friggen achievement when you can get the feels out of a Soviet apocalypse war hero in bug power armor, and do it with such regularity.

+1, just…+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not only is this a good tale in its own right, but it reminded me of 2273, and I appreciate that. :D He's a good guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing these out. I couldn't think of a good way to phrase the good Doctor's death. I'm still undecided on it. I'll fix the typo when I get home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh damn, my bad. I always do this with dates. I'm on mobile so I won't be able to fix it until I get home. Thank you for pointing that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think death by natural causes is suited to be described as termination, unless they really were put out of their misery.

In which case, "euthanise" would probably be a better word.

Nitpicks aside, I really, really liked this. You've said it's the canon end to Alexei's story and it's one that fits him – he's been weathered and worn down by years of horrific things he's experienced and performed, and the melancholy, bittersweet simplicity of Alexei excising his demons in this way.

There's strong character development and while I really ain't a fan of Broken Veil, it worked for me here. All in all, good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nitpicks: "However, several cancerous tissues had evaded detection and SCP-██████'s condition deteriorated rapidly, resulting in its termination on 11 February 2034." I don't think death by natural causes is suited to be described as termination, unless they really were put out of their misery. And I think the mechanical part's spelled as "axle", not "axel". The latter's the guitarist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: September was deemed to be anomalous all this time and subsequently dropped from our calendars ;D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So beautiful. In the hand of the Mechanized God or not, poor Alexei finally found peace. +1
A song to go with that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted - I have enjoyed Alexei's development over the various tales, and this seems fitting.

One nitpick - 21 August to 13 October is not "less than a month", unless that lifted veil scenario was more serious than I understood!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, if nothing else than for the strong character development of a man desperately seeking penance for the war crimes and atrocities he's committed.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks goes to Rimple, mf99k, Jack Ike, AidenEldritch, Overandover, and anyone else who looked at this while I was crybabying about it in chat this whole weekend. Feels good to finally get a major part of my List off of my List and onto the wiki.

Some author commentary
Hide author commentary

This is absolute, definitive MacCanon on how Alexei dies. All other adventures written about him are alt-universe stuff, or not in alignment with my intentions.

This story takes place in a continuity wherein SCP-2217 forces the Foundation to go public and release all of its least-harmful wards in an effort to save face. There may be more stories about this motley crew, but I have none planned in the immediate future. I can elaborate on anyone's questions, but would prefer people to keep spoilers in collapsibles.

ETA that, for those of you wondering about the URL, this is meant to be a half-assed revival of the "Future Foundation" project that was started and then died about a year and a half ago.

Hide author commentary

I hope you all enjoy it!
